content: Endpoint selection depends on disease area, treatment mechanism, and regulatory pathway. Oncology endpoints: Overall Survival (OS)—time from randomization to death from any cause, gold standard but confounded by subsequent therapies; Progression-Free Survival (PFS)—time to tumor progression or death, widely accepted surrogate; Objective Response Rate (ORR)—proportion with tumor shrinkage ≥30%, used in accelerated approval; Disease Control Rate (DCR)—CR+PR+stable disease. Cardiovascular endpoints: MACE (major adverse cardiovascular events), hospitalization for heart failure. Diabetes: HbA1c reduction (validated surrogate), microvascular complications. Endpoint hierarchies often include primary, key secondary, and exploratory endpoints with multiplicity adjustments.
